hypothesis: H1: Bistability is a symmetry-breaking phase transition
hypothesis_H8: H8: C3 symmetry dominates over Z2 symmetry
finding: Asymmetry continuously shifts basin positions AND biases selection. The asymmetry parameter IS an order parameter for a symmetry-breaking transition. At large |asym|, the basins merge/deform.

asym_neg1_0.classification: DEEP-LOW (both episodes, E~68-69 vs normal LOW ~75)

asym_zero.note: See Phase C data — 20% HAM / 62.5% LN HIGH rate, both basins present

asym_pos0_1.classification: FLIPPED — basin switch between episodes 0 and 1

structural_interpretation: Asymmetry is a continuous control parameter. It breaks the Z-mirror symmetry and shifts the energy landscape. Near zero, bistability is preserved. Large |asym| collapses or deforms the basins. Negative asym → down-basin dominant and E lowered. Positive asym → up-basin dominant with elevation at extremes.

hypothesis_status.H1 (asymmetry biases basin selection): CONFIRMED with correction — also continuously shifts basin values
hypothesis_status.H8 (C3 dominates Z2): Will resolve in Phase F when rotation=120° is tested
